# UC-13.2.6 · ITSI Notable Event Aggregation Rules Engine and Correlation Search Health (aggregation policy throughput, correlation skip and runtime percentiles, episode backlog versus notables, dispatch failures, group-by misconfiguration signals, threshold-template runtime stress, modular input restarts, KV-backed policy latency)

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Expert &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Reliability, Performance &middot; **Wave:** Run &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch a busy sorting hall where notices are grouped into batches before anyone acts. When belts skip, doors jam, or the room keeps rebooting, we raise a clear signal so crews fix the machinery before piles land in the wrong bins and customers wait.*

---

## Description

Detects failures, drift, and saturation in the Splunk ITSI Event Analytics rules engine by correlating correlation-search scheduler skip rate and runtime percentiles with notable and episode creation velocities, dispatch failure rates, modular input restart counts, KV-backed lag proxies, and resource hints, then enriching rows with governance lookups for routing.

## Value

Episode managers and platform owners shorten time to innocence when Episode Review floods or goes quiet because they can see whether correlation schedules, aggregation throughput, dispatch queues, or modular inputs are the bottleneck rather than guessing at application regressions. Reliability leaders defend operational review cadences with evidence that ties NEAP behavior to measurable scheduler and dispatcher metrics, while auditors receive repeatable CSV exports that show who owns each correlation object and which business service is impacted.

## Implementation

Schedule uc_13_2_6_itsi_rules_engine_health on the ITSI search head with earliest=-4h@h; forward _internal scheduler and notable dispatch lanes, itsi_tracked_alerts, itsi_grouped_alerts, and _introspection splunk_resource_usage; maintain itsi_correlation_search_inventory.csv and cmdb sourcetype=itsi_policy_inventory; page on severity critical or high with forty-five minute dedupe unless escalation occurs; route by owner_team and attach Episode Review, NEAP, correlation search, and Monitoring Console drilldowns.

## Evidence

Saved search uc_13_2_6_itsi_rules_engine_health with five or ten minute schedule, exported CSV of alert rows joined to itsi_correlation_search_inventory.csv, and ticketed screenshots from Episode Review and correlation search editor for the same correlation_search_name.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

In a lab ITSI tenant, pause a non-production ITSI correlation search briefly to force scheduler skip language, confirm uc_13_2_6_itsi_rules_engine_health returns severity high or critical for the correlation_search_name within twenty minutes with non-null recommended_action and enriched owner_team when inventory rows exist.

### Negative scenario

After restoring schedules and clearing synthetic load, when corr_search_skip_rate stays below two percent, dispatch_failure_rate is zero, modular_input_restart_count is zero for two hours, and introspection is quiet, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for canary correlation searches under test.

## Detailed Implementation

### Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the reliability engineering leads who operate Splunk IT Service Intelligence Event Analytics, the search administrators who schedule correlation searches on production search heads, and the episode managers who curate Notable Event Aggregation Policy objects in governed namespaces. This use case is not a substitute for ITSI installation or licensing work; it assumes Event Analytics is already creating notables, grouping them into episodes, and executing rule actions that may change severity, add comments, or open tickets in external systems. You are monitoring the rules engine plane itself: aggregation policy throughput, correlation search scheduler health, episode creation backlog, dispatcher failures, group-by misconfiguration symptoms, threshold-template runtime errors, modular input stability for itsi_event_management style pipelines, and Key Value store pressure that backs deepdive_kv collections such as itsi_notable_event_aggregation_policy.

Sibling scope keeps reviewers oriented. UC-13.2.1 remains the Service Health Score regression narrative that correlates itsi_summary health_score curves to NEAP episode pressure and KPI skip hints. UC-13.2.2 remains the per-KPI degradation burn-rate story that emphasizes alert_severity crossings, density-style residuals on KPI values, and tracked-alert density. This document is deliberately distinct: it targets the machinery that turns raw notables into grouped episodes and executes correlation searches, not the numeric health of individual KPI cards or composite service health scores. When Service Health looks stable while Episode Review floods or goes silent, the failure mode often lives here in aggregation policy evaluation, correlation search skips, or dispatcher saturation.

Architecture literacy is prerequisite. ITSI Event Analytics ingests notables from correlation searches, direct notable creation APIs, and related pipelines, then applies aggregation policies that decide smart grouping, time windows, similarity thresholds, and episode lifecycle transitions. Correlation searches differ from KPI base searches: correlation searches typically run on a schedule against summary or raw indexes to emit notables when SPL predicates match, while KPI base searches materialize entity-level measurements that feed service scores. NEAP rules reference group-by attributes that must exist on incoming notables; when a policy references a field your data never carries, grouping degenerates into singleton episodes or silent drops that look like mysteriously low episode rates. Threshold templates inherit variables across services; a missing template variable surfaces at correlation runtime as evaluation errors that may skip schedules without a crisp UI banner on every dashboard.

The deepdive_kv subsystem persists aggregation policy definitions and related objects in collections such as itsi_notable_event_aggregation_policy. Heavy write contention or undersized Key Value store capacity increases latency between notable creation and episode materialization, which shows up as rising lag between itsi_tracked_alerts arrival and itsi_grouped_alerts episode rows. Dispatcher components and notable_event_actions style pipelines must dequeue work promptly; when dispatch failure rates climb, analysts still see raw notables while episode queues stall. Modular inputs that implement REST polling or streaming integrations can enter crash loops after upgrades when TLS trust stores, OAuth tokens, or capability maps change; each restart clears in-memory queues briefly and can duplicate dispatches if at-least-once behavior is not reconciled with downstream ticketing.

Search head clustering caveat: ITSI correlation searches and Event Analytics workloads should run on a consistent captain-aware schedule distribution. Rolling restarts across cluster members reorder saved search slots and can spike skipped correlation searches for minutes even when the underlying data plane is healthy. Prefer capturing host or server identity as search_head_id in telemetry so you can separate true saturation from benign failover noise. Indexer lag on itsi_summary or alert indexes upstream of correlation searches can inflate correlation runtime percentiles; treat summary index freshness as a first-class dependency before blaming NEAP configuration.

Role-based access for REST inspection of aggregation policies, correlation searches, and modular inputs typically requires elevated ITSI roles on the search head. Never embed personal credentials in saved searches. Use a dedicated automation account with read-only inventory access for lookups and a separate break-glass account for remediation. Legal reviewers may require masking business_service labels that embed customer names; keep governance fields in CSV inventories rather than in free-text log lines when possible.

Risk briefing: tuning this alert too aggressively pages during every content-pack import or scheduled NEAP backfill. Pair alert outputs with change metadata and throttle duplicate correlation_search_name rows unless severity escalates from medium to high. Executive stakeholders care about episode MTTR and signal-to-noise; this control exists to prove whether the rules engine is keeping pace with incoming notables, not to replace human triage in Episode Review.

Finally, align visualization owners. Monitoring Console, ITSI module dashboards, and custom glass tables should deep link from recommended_action tokens to the same Service Analyzer, Episode Review, and correlation search editor screens that operators already trust. Evidence packs should include exported CSV rows joined to inventory lookups for audit replay.

### Step 2 — Configure data collection

Treat Event Analytics telemetry as five parallel lanes plus inventory lookups, not as a single sourcetype miracle.

Lane scheduler targets index=_internal with sourcetype=scheduler, filtering savedsearch_name to ITSI correlation searches using a conservative substring match on itsi. Retain host or splunk_server as search_head_id, savedsearch_name as correlation_search_name, status and _raw text for skip language, and numeric run_time style fields when your Splunk version exposes them. Increase retention on _internal for at least one week on search heads that run ITSI so weekend incidents still have scheduler forensics.

Lane tracked reads index=itsi_tracked_alerts for notable creation velocity and optional correlation metadata when those fields exist in your deployment. Normalize aggregation_policy_id style keys when present; when absent, allow the SPL to bucket rows under tracked_lane so the control still measures global ingest pressure.

Lane grouped reads index=itsi_grouped_alerts for episode creation velocity, NEAP identifiers, and lag proxies using _indextime minus _time when both are trustworthy. Flatten nested JSON at ingest if your forwarder path produces multiline episodes so dc(group_id) style math stays honest at search time.

Lane dispatch reads index=_internal focusing on source patterns associated with itsi notable event dispatch, notable_event_actions, and itsi_event_management modular input narratives. Capture failure and throttle language, HTTP 429 style tokens when present, and restart verbs that imply modular input churn. If your estate uses different source paths, document the mapping in props.conf and adjust the search filter with change control.

Lane introspection reads index=_introspection with sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage, filtering component fields that include ITSI substrings. This lane provides CPU and memory hints for rules-engine-adjacent processes when introspection is enabled. Disable the lane in estates that forbid introspection indexing, and document the blind spot in your risk register.

Maintain lookup table itsi_correlation_search_inventory.csv keyed by correlation_search_name with columns owner_team, business_service, and criticality. Refresh from configuration management after every correlation search import. Maintain index=cmdb sourcetype=itsi_policy_inventory as a CMDB-aligned export keyed by correlation_search_name with deduped rows for join enrichment. If cmdb is unavailable, materialize a nightly REST export into the same sourcetype using a hardened modular input.

props.conf and transforms.conf should preserve precise _time on itsi_tracked_alerts and itsi_grouped_alerts. Confirm TIME_PREFIX on _internal scheduler lines so skip bursts align with NEAP windows. When forwarding _internal off search heads to an observability cluster, verify that host fields remain distinguishable per origin search head.

Optional KV collection monitoring: when permitted, schedule a low-frequency rest query or custom alert that samples collection sizes and update rates for itsi_notable_event_aggregation_policy without dumping full documents into indexes. Store only counters and hashes in summaries to respect data minimization policies.

Volume controls: high-frequency correlation searches can dominate scheduler logs. Use summary indexing for hourly rollups of skip rates if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal objectives. Kubernetes-hosted Splunk should pin resource requests for search and KV workloads so CPU throttling is not misread as pure SPL inefficiency.

### Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as uc_13_2_6_itsi_rules_engine_health with a five or ten minute schedule depending on organizational noise tolerance, dispatch earliest=-4h@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is critical or high. Throttle duplicate correlation_search_name and aggregation_policy_id pairs for forty-five minutes unless severity escalates. Include recommended_action tokens in pager bodies and attach Episode Review, aggregation policy editor, correlation search editor, and Monitoring Console drilldowns.

Pipeline narrative: multisearch fans scheduler, tracked, grouped, dispatch, and introspection arms so one silent lane cannot hide a saturated rules engine. streamstats computes short-window maxima for skip rate, dispatch failure rate, and modular input restart counts per correlation_search_name and aggregation_policy_id. eventstats adds fleet median context for skip and runtime baselines. case maps combined signals into severity tiers aligned with operational policy. inputlookup and cmdb join add owner_team and business_service for routing.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.6 ITSI rules engine NEAP aggregation correlation scheduler dispatch introspection earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval sv=lower(trim(toString(savedsearch_name)))
      | where like(sv,"%itsi%")
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"sh_unknown")))
      | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(savedsearch_name))
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString("na_policy"))
      | eval skip_hit=if(match(lower(_raw),"skip|deferred|did not complete|missed") OR like(status,"skip%"),1,0)
      | eval run_ms=tonumber(coalesce(run_time,run_duration,execute_time,0))
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats count AS sruns sum(skip_hit) AS sskip perc95(run_ms) AS rtp95 BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=if(sruns>0,round(100*sskip/sruns,3),0)
      | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=round(coalesce(rtp95,0),2)
      | eval notable_per_min=0 | eval episode_per_min=0 | eval dispatch_failure_rate=0 | eval modular_input_restart_count=0 | eval kv_store_lag_ms=0
      | eval lane="arm_sched"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"itsi_sh")))
      | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(coalesce(correlation_search,corr_search,correlation_search_name,"tracked_lane")))
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString(coalesce(aggregation_policy_id,notable_agg_policy,"")))
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats count AS tcnt BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval notable_per_min=round(tcnt/10,3)
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=0 | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=0 | eval episode_per_min=0 | eval dispatch_failure_rate=0 | eval modular_input_restart_count=0 | eval kv_store_lag_ms=0
      | eval lane="arm_tracked"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"itsi_sh")))
      | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(coalesce(correlation_search_name,source_correlation,"grouped_lane")))
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString(coalesce(aggregation_policy_id,policy_id,neap_policy_id,"")))
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats count AS gcnt max(eval(if(isnotnull(_indextime) AND isnotnull(_time),(_indextime-_time)*1000,0))) AS kv_store_lag_ms BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval episode_per_min=round(gcnt/10,3)
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=0 | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=0 | eval notable_per_min=0 | eval dispatch_failure_rate=0 | eval modular_input_restart_count=0
      | eval lane="arm_grouped"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
    [ search index=_internal earliest=-4h@h latest=now (source=*itsi_notable_event* OR source=*notable_event_actions* OR source=*itsi_event_management*)
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"sh_unknown")))
      | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(coalesce(correlation_search_name,correlation_search,"dispatch_lane")))
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString(coalesce(aggregation_policy_id,policy_id,"")))
      | eval lx=lower(_raw)
      | eval fail_hit=if(match(lx,"dispatch.*fail|failed to dispatch|error while dispatch|throttl|429"),1,0)
      | eval restart_hit=if(match(lx,"modular input.*restart|restarting|started.*itsi_event_management|stopped.*itsi_event_management"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats sum(fail_hit) AS fh sum(restart_hit) AS rh count AS ch BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval dispatch_failure_rate=if(ch>0,round(100*fh/ch,3),0)
      | eval modular_input_restart_count=rh
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=0 | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=0 | eval notable_per_min=0 | eval episode_per_min=0 | eval kv_store_lag_ms=0
      | eval lane="arm_dispatch"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval comp=lower(trim(toString(component)))
      | where like(comp,"%itsi%")
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,data.host)))
      | eval correlation_search_name="_itsi_resource_lane"
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=""
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats perc95(data.cpu_system_pct) AS c95 max(data.mem_used) AS mmax BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval kv_store_lag_ms=round(if(isnotnull(mmax) AND mmax>0,mmax/1024,0),3)
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=0 | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=round(coalesce(c95,0)*1000,2)
      | eval notable_per_min=0 | eval episode_per_min=0 | eval dispatch_failure_rate=0 | eval modular_input_restart_count=0
      | eval lane="arm_intro"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
| eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(search_head_id,"sh_unknown")))
| eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(coalesce(correlation_search_name,"__row__")))
| eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString(coalesce(aggregation_policy_id,"")))
| sort 0 search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id - _time
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f max(corr_search_skip_rate) AS css3 max(dispatch_failure_rate) AS dfr3 max(modular_input_restart_count) AS mic3 BY search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
| eventstats median(corr_search_skip_rate) AS skip_med median(corr_search_runtime_p95) AS rt_med
| eval severity=case((coalesce(css3,corr_search_skip_rate)>10) OR (coalesce(dfr3,dispatch_failure_rate)>5) OR (coalesce(mic3,modular_input_restart_count)>=2),"critical",(coalesce(css3,corr_search_skip_rate)>5) OR (coalesce(dfr3,dispatch_failure_rate)>2) OR (coalesce(corr_search_runtime_p95,0)>120000),"high",(coalesce(css3,corr_search_skip_rate)>2) OR (coalesce(notable_per_min,0)>500) OR (coalesce(episode_per_min,0)>200) OR (coalesce(kv_store_lag_ms,0)>7500),"medium",true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","page_rules_engine_owner_clear_scheduler_resize_kv_open_neap_correlation_and_threshold_template_drills",severity=="high","triage_correlation_runtime_summary_index_lag_and_service_now_action_connectors",severity=="medium","review_aggregation_policy_group_by_fields_and_rest_modinput_throttle_limits",true(),"continue_monitoring_rules_engine_capacity_and_episode_auto_close_policies")
| join type=left correlation_search_name
    [| inputlookup itsi_correlation_search_inventory.csv
     | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(correlation_search_name))
     | fields correlation_search_name owner_team business_service ]
| join type=left correlation_search_name
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=itsi_policy_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
     | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(correlation_search_name))
     | dedup correlation_search_name
     | rename owner_team AS owner_team_cmdb
     | rename business_service AS business_service_cmdb
     | fields correlation_search_name owner_team_cmdb business_service_cmdb ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,owner_team_cmdb,"unowned")
| eval business_service=coalesce(business_service,business_service_cmdb,"unspecified_bizsvc")
| table _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id owner_team business_service corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[uc_13_2_6_itsi_rules_engine_health_alert]
cron_schedule = */10 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch uc_13_2_6_itsi_rules_engine_health | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance guidance: materialize each multisearch arm into a summary index on ten-minute buckets when search concurrency is tight. Keep this full search for investigations and point operational alerts at summaries with earliest=-2h@h.

### Step 4 — Validate

Begin in a lab tenant. Induce a controlled correlation search skip by temporarily pausing a non-production ITSI correlation search, confirm scheduler lines show skip language, and verify corr_search_skip_rate rises within two windows while owner_team enrichment still resolves from itsi_correlation_search_inventory.csv.

Validate NEAP throughput by forcing a short burst of related synthetic notables that should aggregate into one episode, confirm episode_per_min and notable_per_min move together in Splunk within the same ten-minute bins visible in Episode Review.

Validate dispatch arm sensitivity by injecting safe failure narratives into a sandbox forwarder path or by replaying redacted _internal snippets in a test index; confirm dispatch_failure_rate responds and recommended_action points to connector and throttle checks rather than generic infrastructure blame.

Validate join paths by running inputlookup itsi_correlation_search_inventory.csv alone and confirming correlation_search_name coverage for tier-zero paths. Run the cmdb subsearch alone and confirm dedup correlation_search_name returns one row per correlation object.

Validate introspection arm by confirming index=_introspection contains splunk_resource_usage rows with ITSI components on at least one search head in non-production. If introspection is disabled, document the blind spot and rely on scheduler and dispatch lanes only after risk acceptance.

Negative validation: after recovery, when skip rates fall below two percent, dispatch failures are zero for sixty minutes, and modular input restart counts are zero, filtering severity to critical and high should return zero rows for canary correlation searches.

Synthetic migration validation: after search head cluster failover drills, compare search_head_id slices to captain metadata and confirm alert noise returns to baseline within thirty minutes; if not, tune streamstats window size with change control.

### Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Aggregation policy group-by references a missing field: Episode Review shows singleton floods while NEAP similarity looks idle; open the aggregation policy editor, validate smart group fields against live notable samples, fix field aliases at ingest, and replay a canary notable batch before closing the ticket.

Case 2 — Key Value store under-provisioned causing episode lag: kv_store_lag_ms climbs while grouped alert arrival trails tracked alerts; expand KV capacity, reduce hot collection churn, verify deepdive_kv replication on clustered search heads, and schedule off-peak maintenance for compaction.

Case 3 — Correlation search timeouts due to summary index lag: corr_search_runtime_p95 spikes while indexer fixup stories show catch-up; prioritize itsi_summary freshness, widen earliest windows temporarily with governance approval, and split correlation searches that scan excessive raw history.

Case 4 — Scheduler queue full: corr_search_skip_rate rises without dispatcher failures; inspect concurrent scheduled search counts, move heavy ad hoc workloads, verify ITSI maintenance windows, and rebalance captain scheduling on search head clusters.

Case 5 — Modular input crash loop after upgrade: modular_input_restart_count stays above zero with rolling ERROR lines; roll forward to a patched maintenance release, reconcile Python and OpenSSL dependencies on heavy forwarders, and validate OAuth secrets for REST modular inputs.

Case 6 — Rule action ServiceNow connection failure: dispatch_failure_rate climbs with HTTP errors while skips stay low; test mid-server connectivity, rotate integration credentials, validate outbound proxy allow lists, and pause non-critical actions to protect queue depth.

Case 7 — Threshold template variable missing: correlation searches log evaluation errors and skips cluster on specific services; open threshold template inheritance, restore missing macro tokens, republish templates to dependent services, and backfill validation searches on a canary service.

Case 8 — Dispatcher duplicates after migration: episode counts double with paired tickets; verify dedupe keys in aggregation policies, reconcile at-least-once dispatch with ticketing idempotency tokens, and replay NEAP similarity thresholds with migration runbooks.

Case 9 — Episode auto-close not firing: grouped indexes grow while severity never decays; validate auto-close windows, confirm close actions are not blocked by external workflow states, and inspect correlation actions for exceptions swallowed into internal logs.

Case 10 — Content pack import overwriting customizations: inventory joins suddenly show unowned teams; restore CSV backups, reapply local overrides outside pack paths, and diff correlation_search definitions before re-enabling alerts.

Case 11 — REST throttling on REST modular input: dispatch_failure_rate shows 429 language; reduce poll frequency, batch REST calls, negotiate higher quotas, and add exponential backoff in modular input configuration where supported.

Case 12 — Dispatch latency from cross-search-head distribution: identical policies diverge by search_head_id; align saved search replication, verify deployer pushes, eliminate ad hoc local edits on single members, and confirm captain runs the canonical schedules.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, and inputlookup appear; final table lists fifteen columns including owner_team and business_service.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.2.6 ITSI rules engine NEAP aggregation correlation scheduler dispatch introspection earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval sv=lower(trim(toString(savedsearch_name)))
      | where like(sv,"%itsi%")
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"sh_unknown")))
      | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(savedsearch_name))
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString("na_policy"))
      | eval skip_hit=if(match(lower(_raw),"skip|deferred|did not complete|missed") OR like(status,"skip%"),1,0)
      | eval run_ms=tonumber(coalesce(run_time,run_duration,execute_time,0))
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats count AS sruns sum(skip_hit) AS sskip perc95(run_ms) AS rtp95 BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=if(sruns>0,round(100*sskip/sruns,3),0)
      | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=round(coalesce(rtp95,0),2)
      | eval notable_per_min=0 | eval episode_per_min=0 | eval dispatch_failure_rate=0 | eval modular_input_restart_count=0 | eval kv_store_lag_ms=0
      | eval lane="arm_sched"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_tracked_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"itsi_sh")))
      | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(coalesce(correlation_search,corr_search,correlation_search_name,"tracked_lane")))
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString(coalesce(aggregation_policy_id,notable_agg_policy,"")))
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats count AS tcnt BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval notable_per_min=round(tcnt/10,3)
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=0 | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=0 | eval episode_per_min=0 | eval dispatch_failure_rate=0 | eval modular_input_restart_count=0 | eval kv_store_lag_ms=0
      | eval lane="arm_tracked"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_grouped_alerts earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"itsi_sh")))
      | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(coalesce(correlation_search_name,source_correlation,"grouped_lane")))
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString(coalesce(aggregation_policy_id,policy_id,neap_policy_id,"")))
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats count AS gcnt max(eval(if(isnotnull(_indextime) AND isnotnull(_time),(_indextime-_time)*1000,0))) AS kv_store_lag_ms BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval episode_per_min=round(gcnt/10,3)
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=0 | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=0 | eval notable_per_min=0 | eval dispatch_failure_rate=0 | eval modular_input_restart_count=0
      | eval lane="arm_grouped"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
    [ search index=_internal earliest=-4h@h latest=now (source=*itsi_notable_event* OR source=*notable_event_actions* OR source=*itsi_event_management*)
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server,"sh_unknown")))
      | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(coalesce(correlation_search_name,correlation_search,"dispatch_lane")))
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString(coalesce(aggregation_policy_id,policy_id,"")))
      | eval lx=lower(_raw)
      | eval fail_hit=if(match(lx,"dispatch.*fail|failed to dispatch|error while dispatch|throttl|429"),1,0)
      | eval restart_hit=if(match(lx,"modular input.*restart|restarting|started.*itsi_event_management|stopped.*itsi_event_management"),1,0)
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats sum(fail_hit) AS fh sum(restart_hit) AS rh count AS ch BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval dispatch_failure_rate=if(ch>0,round(100*fh/ch,3),0)
      | eval modular_input_restart_count=rh
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=0 | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=0 | eval notable_per_min=0 | eval episode_per_min=0 | eval kv_store_lag_ms=0
      | eval lane="arm_dispatch"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval comp=lower(trim(toString(component)))
      | where like(comp,"%itsi%")
      | eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(host,data.host)))
      | eval correlation_search_name="_itsi_resource_lane"
      | eval aggregation_policy_id=""
      | bin _time span=10m
      | stats perc95(data.cpu_system_pct) AS c95 max(data.mem_used) AS mmax BY _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
      | eval kv_store_lag_ms=round(if(isnotnull(mmax) AND mmax>0,mmax/1024,0),3)
      | eval corr_search_skip_rate=0 | eval corr_search_runtime_p95=round(coalesce(c95,0)*1000,2)
      | eval notable_per_min=0 | eval episode_per_min=0 | eval dispatch_failure_rate=0 | eval modular_input_restart_count=0
      | eval lane="arm_intro"
      | fields _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms lane ]
| eval search_head_id=trim(toString(coalesce(search_head_id,"sh_unknown")))
| eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(coalesce(correlation_search_name,"__row__")))
| eval aggregation_policy_id=trim(toString(coalesce(aggregation_policy_id,"")))
| sort 0 search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id - _time
| streamstats window=3 current=t global=f max(corr_search_skip_rate) AS css3 max(dispatch_failure_rate) AS dfr3 max(modular_input_restart_count) AS mic3 BY search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id
| eventstats median(corr_search_skip_rate) AS skip_med median(corr_search_runtime_p95) AS rt_med
| eval severity=case((coalesce(css3,corr_search_skip_rate)>10) OR (coalesce(dfr3,dispatch_failure_rate)>5) OR (coalesce(mic3,modular_input_restart_count)>=2),"critical",(coalesce(css3,corr_search_skip_rate)>5) OR (coalesce(dfr3,dispatch_failure_rate)>2) OR (coalesce(corr_search_runtime_p95,0)>120000),"high",(coalesce(css3,corr_search_skip_rate)>2) OR (coalesce(notable_per_min,0)>500) OR (coalesce(episode_per_min,0)>200) OR (coalesce(kv_store_lag_ms,0)>7500),"medium",true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(severity=="critical","page_rules_engine_owner_clear_scheduler_resize_kv_open_neap_correlation_and_threshold_template_drills",severity=="high","triage_correlation_runtime_summary_index_lag_and_service_now_action_connectors",severity=="medium","review_aggregation_policy_group_by_fields_and_rest_modinput_throttle_limits",true(),"continue_monitoring_rules_engine_capacity_and_episode_auto_close_policies")
| join type=left correlation_search_name
    [| inputlookup itsi_correlation_search_inventory.csv
     | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(correlation_search_name))
     | fields correlation_search_name owner_team business_service ]
| join type=left correlation_search_name
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=itsi_policy_inventory earliest=-24h latest=now
     | eval correlation_search_name=trim(toString(correlation_search_name))
     | dedup correlation_search_name
     | rename owner_team AS owner_team_cmdb
     | rename business_service AS business_service_cmdb
     | fields correlation_search_name owner_team_cmdb business_service_cmdb ]
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,owner_team_cmdb,"unowned")
| eval business_service=coalesce(business_service,business_service_cmdb,"unspecified_bizsvc")
| table _time search_head_id correlation_search_name aggregation_policy_id owner_team business_service corr_search_skip_rate corr_search_runtime_p95 notable_per_min episode_per_min dispatch_failure_rate modular_input_restart_count kv_store_lag_ms severity recommended_action
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Alerts.severity) AS alert_sev latest(Alerts.signature) AS alert_sig FROM datamodel=Alerts WHERE nodename=Alerts earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Alerts.dest
| rename Alerts.dest AS rules_dest
| join type=left max=0 rules_dest [| tstats summariesonly=t latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS rules_dest ]
| table rules_dest alert_sig alert_sev cpu_load mem_used
```

## Visualization

Episode Review queue depth tiles, correlation search health tables colored by severity, scheduler skip sparklines by search_head_id, dispatch failure stacked bars, KV lag histograms, and owner_team routing panels with drilldowns to aggregation policy editors.

## Known False Positives

Planned content-pack imports temporarily reorder correlation searches and can spike scheduler skips until bundles settle; require change metadata before paging application owners. Scheduled NEAP backfill jobs replay historical notables and can inflate notable_per_min without live incident pressure; gate medium severity during documented backfill windows. IIS or mid-tier outages for ticketing webhooks can raise dispatch_failure_rate while the rules engine itself is healthy; confirm external service health before blaming aggregation policies. ITSI version upgrades restart modular inputs and can flash high restart counts for minutes; compare against release notes and delay pages until rolling restarts finish. Search head cluster rolling restarts reorder correlation schedules and can elevate corr_search_skip_rate briefly; validate captain stability and suppress duplicates using search_head_id throttles. Quarterly disaster exercises may intentionally throttle REST inputs; annotate drill labels in inventory lookups so severity downgrades are auditable. Mis-tuned introspection sampling can emit sparse resource lanes; do not infer CPU truth from empty introspection during maintenance. Stale itsi_correlation_search_inventory.csv rows route pages to the wrong owner_team until configuration management refreshes lookups. False calm appears when _internal retention is shorter than the alert window; extend retention or lower earliest bounds after risk review. Duplicate cmdb rows despite dedup indicate upstream feed errors; fix cmdb pipelines before muting join failures.

- [Splunk Docs — Saved search scheduling and concurrency](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Report/)
- [Splunk Docs — splunk_resource_usage introspection](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Troubleshoot/)
